rows.notes: Very clear top-line carried balance. Numbers 145, 16, 0 and a half-penny loop are well aligned in the three currency columns.

rows.description: Shiers an acct book for Hare clipped scholarship
rows.notes: Description partly faded but legible enough. Amount reads as two digits aligned in the shillings and pence columns (6s 6d). Alignment with column lines is clear.
Short description readable. Single small figure '2' sits under the shillings column (no pence), interpreted as 2s 0d. Slight uncertainty whether a faint pence digit exists; none visible.
Description is legible. Three-column amount clearly reads 9 (pounds), 16 (shillings), 3 (pence). All values within valid ranges.

rows.description: Mr Shepart Provisional Stipend to 2 Days
rows.notes: Handwriting clear. Amount aligns to pounds and shillings columns as 12£ 10s, no pence indicated.

rows.description: Insurance / other charge (partially struck through, illegible)
rows.notes: This line is heavily struck through and the description is not legible; no clear amount visible. Marked as an entry because it appears to be a transaction line.
Description and three-part amount (4£ 11s 8d) are clearly written and well aligned with the currency columns.

rows.description: Subscription to Tileyford Church St Germans
rows.notes: Single large number '30' sits in the pounds column (shillings would be invalid at 30). Description legible.

rows.description: Proportion of expense of Tithe Commutation at Gessing(?)
rows.notes: Description partly uncertain (place name unclear). Amount appears as two figures; interpreted as 9£ 5s because of alignment with the pounds column; advisable to review original if exact place is required.

rows.description: Mr Rawlinson journey to Tor on Coll business
rows.notes: Name and purpose readable with minor letter ambiguity. Amount aligns as 2£ 10s. Could be 2s10d if misaligned, but alignment favors pounds/shillings.

rows.description: Mr Harris Kingsbridge bill for exchange at Woodleigh
rows.notes: Handwriting clear; amount in pounds and shillings (11£ 15s) is well aligned and legible.

rows.description: Mr King Solicitor Blandford bill relating to Shillingstone exchange
rows.notes: Large single number '60' appears in the pounds column. Description readable. 60£ fits alignment and is valid.

rows.description: Mr Kirkland transcribing & locating and quarter of the clerk(?)
rows.notes: Description has uncertain words (transcribing & locating phrase is readable but latter words unclear). Number '15' aligns in the pounds column; could possibly be shillings but pounds position is more likely here.
Description legible. The numeric '16' is relatively small and sits in the shillings column visually; interpreted as 16s 0d. Slight uncertainty due to cramped digits.
Text readable; amount appears as two figures aligned to pounds and shillings (2£ 15s). Alignment of the first digit falls under pounds column.
This line is a group introduction (uses 'viz' and is followed by indented sub-items). No monetary amount on this line.
Indented sub-item under Faulkner repairs. The old-style notation '£3.8.6' is clear and maps directly to 3£ 8s 6d.
Indented minor item. The '6' is clearly a shillings amount (6s) in the shillings column; description short but legible.
Indented painter bill written in the traditional format '1.3.3' — read as 1£ 3s 3d; clear and aligned.
Indented item '1.19' interpreted as 1£ 19s. Handwritten spacing makes pence unclear (no pence digit); 19 shillings is valid and fits alignment.
A subtotal follows the grouped items; reads as 12£ 11s. Alignment and context (sum after indented items) support this being a total.

rows.description: Mr Shepart Provisl Stipend to Midson
rows.notes: Very similar phrasing to earlier Shepart stipend line; amount 12£ 10s clearly aligned.

rows.description: Mr Harrington repairs to N. Newton Channel
rows.notes: Amount shows three-part figure with a half-penny sign after the pence (read as 2£ 7s 5½d). The halfpenny loop is visible but slightly faint.
Description a little cramped but readable. Amount aligns as 1£ 10s.
Name and purpose readable; amount aligns to pounds and shillings (2£ 10s).
This ledger line has been heavily struck through and cannot be read; no clear amount visible. Marked as entry because it appears in the transaction area.
Description partly uncertain (surname reading), but '33 Instalment' appears; the figure '25' sits clearly in pounds column. Could be an instalment number reference rather than amount.
Another Shepart stipend entry; amount 12£ 10s is clearly visible. Some words in description are abbreviated and slightly unclear.

rows.description: Mr Rose allowance at 5/7 per week 44 weeks from Xmas 1843 to Oct 28 1844
rows.notes: Long descriptive line; the single figure '11' is visible and sits in the pounds column. There is some ambiguity whether '11' correlates to the whole allowance or a subtotal, but alignment suggests pounds.
This is a grouping header ('viz') introducing subsequent detailed bills. No amount on the header line itself.

rows.description: Bills paid by the [agent] at Gossington £59.0.2
rows.notes: Indented detailed bill after the 'viz' header. Written as traditional notation '£59.0.2' which is clear and well aligned.
Clear notation '43.3.4' for a general bills entry; alignment and digits are crisp.
Sum of the two immediately preceding items. The printed subtotal '102 12 6' is clearly written and lines up with the currency columns.

rows.description: Mr Bunker on acct of Shillingstone exchange
rows.notes: Name and purpose reasonably legible; amount '58 12' sits in pounds and shillings columns respectively (58£ 12s).
Annual bill explicitly written as '38.5.8' (38£ 5s 8d). Clear and aligned.

rows.description: Charges in regard to tithe claim £89.4.0
rows.notes: Line reads as '89.4' in the pounds/shillings columns; pence not written and assumed 0d. Description is legible but slightly cramped.
A combined total '127 9 8' appears after the Walsh and tithe charge lines. Clear alignment and digits; arithmetic corresponds to the two prior amounts (38.5.8 + 89.4.0 = 127.9.8).
Final total at bottom of page reads as 676£ 4s 1d. Figures are large and well aligned under the currency columns.
